req.title: Device data ingest (audit)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates three distinct behavioral zones: (1) registered devices successfully ingesting FHIR Observations with normalization and AuditEvent emission (positive / EP), (2) unregistered or cert-invalid devices receiving HTTP 401 with a corresponding AuditEvent (negative / NEG), and (3) a boundary case at the exact transition point between registered and unregistered state — a device whose registration has just been revoked — exercising the State Transition from 'registered' to 'deregistered' (STC). A fourth Decision Table case covers the combination matrix of cert validity × device registration to ensure all rule intersections are exercised. Together these cases achieve full coverage of the stated acceptance criteria, error pathways, and audit obligations.

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_stores_observation_and_emits_audit
preconditions: Device 'dev-001' is registered in fhir-lite device registry
Valid TLS client certificate for 'dev-001' is available
FHIR Observation payload conforms to R4 schema
AuditEvent store is accessible via /AuditEvent endpoint
actions: POST a valid FHIR Observation to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
Record the returned resource ID from the 201 response
expected: Observation retrievable with normalized units (e.g. kg -> g where applicable)
At least one AuditEvent exists referencing the Observation resource ID
trace: R-HLT-097: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_registered_device_ingest_stores_observation_and_emits_audit(
    registered_device_cert, base_url, fhir_observation_payload, audit_client
):
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest",
        json=fhir_observation_payload(device_id="dev-001"),
        cert=registered_device_cert("dev-001"),
        verify=False,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    resource_id = resp.json()["id"]

    obs_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Observation/{resource_id}",
        cert=registered_device_cert("dev-001"),
        verify=False,
    )
    assert obs_resp.status_code == 200
    obs_body = obs_resp.json()
    assert obs_body.get("id") == resource_id
    assert <normalize_check>(obs_body), "Normalization not applied"

    audit_events = audit_client.query(entity_id=resource_id)
    assert len(audit_events) >= 1, "No AuditEvent emitted"
    assert audit_events[0]["outcome"] == "0", "AuditEvent outcome not success"

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_cert_rejected_with_401_and_audit
preconditions: Device 'dev-bad' is NOT registered in fhir-lite device registry
A TLS client certificate exists for 'dev-bad' but is unknown to the server
actions: POST a FHIR Observation to /Ingest using the unregistered device certificate
Capture HTTP response code and body
Query /AuditEvent for any event referencing the attempted ingest
expected: Response body contains OperationOutcome with issue.code == 'security'
No Observation resource created in the store
AuditEvent emitted with outcome != '0' (failure) for the rejected attempt
trace: R-HLT-097: unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unregistered_device_cert_rejected_with_401_and_audit(
    unregistered_device_cert, base_url, fhir_observation_payload, audit_client
):
    payload = fhir_observation_payload(device_id="dev-bad")
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest",
        json=payload,
        cert=unregistered_device_cert("dev-bad"),
        verify=False,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    issues = body.get("issue", [])
    assert any(i.get("code") == "security" for i in issues), "Missing security OperationOutcome"

    search_resp = requests.get(
        f"{base_url}/Observation?device=dev-bad",
        cert=<admin_cert>,
        verify=False,
    )
    assert search_resp.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "Observation must not be stored"

    audit_events = audit_client.query(agent_id="dev-bad", outcome_not="0")
    assert len(audit_events) >= 1, "Failure AuditEvent must be emitted"

tests.name: test_freshly_deregistered_device_transitions_to_rejected_state
preconditions: Device 'dev-revoke' is currently REGISTERED with a valid certificate
A successful ingest can be performed while registered (pre-condition verification step)
Admin API available to deregister device atomically
actions: POST an Observation with 'dev-revoke' cert while still registered — expect 201
Call admin endpoint to deregister 'dev-revoke' immediately
POST an identical Observation with the same cert within 2 seconds of deregistration
Query AuditEvent store for both attempts
expected: Second POST returns HTTP 401 (deregistered state boundary)
AuditEvent with outcome '0' exists for the first ingest
AuditEvent with failure outcome exists for the second attempt
trace: R-HLT-097: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
R-HLT-097: unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
STC: covers registered -> deregistered state boundary
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
import time

def test_freshly_deregistered_device_transitions_to_rejected_state(
    temp_registered_device, base_url, fhir_observation_payload, admin_client, audit_client
):
    device_id = "dev-revoke"
    cert = temp_registered_device(device_id)

    payload = fhir_observation_payload(device_id=device_id)
    resp_pre = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest", json=payload, cert=cert, verify=False
    )
    assert resp_pre.status_code == 201, "Pre-deregister ingest must succeed"
    resource_id_pre = resp_pre.json()["id"]

    admin_client.deregister_device(device_id)
    time.sleep(0.1)

    resp_post = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest", json=payload, cert=cert, verify=False
    )
    assert resp_post.status_code == 401, f"Post-deregister must return 401, got {resp_post.status_code}"

    audits_ok = audit_client.query(entity_id=resource_id_pre, outcome="0")
    assert len(audits_ok) >= 1, "Success AuditEvent missing for pre-deregister ingest"

    audits_fail = audit_client.query(agent_id=device_id, outcome_not="0")
    assert len(audits_fail) >= 1, "Failure AuditEvent missing for post-deregister attempt"

tests.name: test_decision_table_cert_validity_x_registration_combinations
preconditions: Device 'dev-unreg-valid': NOT registered + valid cert (self-signed, unknown CA)
Device 'dev-reg-invalid': registered entry exists BUT cert is expired/revoked
actions: POST Observation for each device/cert combination to /Ingest
Collect HTTP status codes for all three requests
expected: dev-reg-valid -> HTTP 201 (registered AND valid cert = accept)
dev-unreg-valid -> HTTP 401 (unregistered = reject regardless of cert validity)
dev-reg-invalid -> HTTP 401 (invalid cert = reject regardless of registration)
AuditEvent emitted for every attempt (success and failures)
trace: R-HLT-097: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
R-HLT-097: unregistered devices shall be rejected
R-HLT-097: AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation
DT: covers all cert-validity x registration rule combinations
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

@pytest.mark.parametrize("device_id,cert_fixture,expected_status", [
    ("dev-reg-valid",     "registered_valid_cert",   201),
    ("dev-unreg-valid",   "unregistered_valid_cert",  401),
    ("dev-reg-invalid",   "registered_invalid_cert",  401),
])
def test_decision_table_cert_validity_x_registration_combinations(
    device_id, cert_fixture, expected_status,
    request, base_url, fhir_observation_payload, audit_client
):
    cert = request.getfixturevalue(cert_fixture)(device_id)
    payload = fhir_observation_payload(device_id=device_id)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest", json=payload, cert=cert, verify=False
    )
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, (
        f"[{device_id}] Expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}"
    )

    entity_hint = resp.json().get("id", device_id)
    audits = audit_client.query(agent_id=device_id)
    assert len(audits) >= 1, f"AuditEvent missing for device {device_id}"
